mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity capital relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline and recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this indicates robust aggregate demand and earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand momentum that single-series analysis misses.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is an always-on trend signal. Sustained month-over-month growth in INDPRO indicates expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength, supporting corporate profitability and positive equity returns over 5 trading days as market reprices earnings upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 1-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth does not predict positive 5-day returns in more than 52% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth accelerates while UNRATE declines simultaneously, this signals a tight labor market that supports wage growth, consumer confidence, and corporate earnings. This interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1% AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
failure_condition: Joint labor market tightening signals fail to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3% year-over-year signals monetary policy constraint. When inflation accelerates, the Federal Reserve faces pressure to maintain or raise rates, reducing equity valuations through higher discount rates. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of instances when inflation exceeds 3% year-over-year, or correlation between inflation acceleration and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is an always-on trend signal. Quarterly GDP growth above 2% annualized indicates sustained economic expansion and earnings growth. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth does not predict positive 60-day returns in more than 54% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal broad-based economic expansion. When both series show simultaneous acceleration, this indicates supply-side capacity growth coupled with labor demand, supporting sustained earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures economy-wide momentum.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1%
failure_condition: Joint acceleration signals in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.
